THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF BLUES EXPRESS,INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT COLEMAN, TONY C MITCHELL, CHARLES DBA AS JUS BLUES ENTERTAINMENT COMPANY $44,663.77 PRINCIPAL, $8,932.75 INTEREST, $256.00 COSTS, $30,336.23 LOST PROFITS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $84,188.75, SEE SCANNED DOC. calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-08-2003 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2003 Continued To Oct-20-03 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

Default Prove Up In Scd.
Off Calendar Sep-04-2003 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for hearing this day.
Commissioner Sue Kaplan Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha/V. Mack, CSR: Leticia Perez
Appearances:
Daniel R. Baco, Esq. for Plaintiff Blues Express, Inc., and plaintiff's witness, Beatrice Cummings, also present, were sworn & examined.
No appearances by Defendants.
After hearing the evidence presented, plaintiff's declarations, the Court rules in favor of the plaintiff and against the defendants Tony C. Coleman and Charles Mitchell dba Jus Blues Entertainment Company:
Principal $44,663.77
Costs of $256.00
Lost profits of $30,336.23
Interest of $8,932.75. For the total of $84,188.75.
All causes of action dismissed except the First Cause of Action for Fraud and Deceit. Default Judgment for Fraud and Deceit only.
Judgment signed in open court.

Daniel Ray Bacon represented Plaintiff, Blues Express, Inc.Beatrice Cummings, Treasurer/Director, was sworn and examined on behalf of the Plaintiff.
